This stage is the execution-facing bridge between external requests or tool/task entry points and the actual process-launch machinery. It sits on the boundary between protocol/UI-facing code and the lower-level exec backend, turning JSON-RPC calls, TUI actions, and core tool invocations into sandboxed or unsandboxed command runs, interactive sessions, and streamed output.

On the app-server side, command_exec_processor.rs and command_exec.rs implement the command/exec RPC path, including spawning commands, tracking long-lived executions per connection, forwarding stdin/resize/terminate controls, streaming output, and returning final results. process_exec_processor.rs provides a similar connection-scoped process API with its own in-memory session manager. In the TUI, workspace_command.rs exposes a simple way to run short workspace commands through app-server command/exec, while fs.rs adds companion filesystem RPC helpers.

In core, exec.rs is the main execution engine, and sandboxing/mod.rs converts sandbox-planning decisions into executable requests. user_shell.rs powers the /shell task. The shell and unified-exec handlers and runtimes translate tool arguments into exec requests, apply approval and permission policy, support patch interception and hook payloads, choose backends such as zsh_fork_backend.rs, and finally launch processes through the shared execution path.
